root_text.sn35.167:1.3: “kathaṁ nu kho, bhante, jānato kathaṁ passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyatī”ti?
root_text.sn35.167:1.4: “Cakkhuṁ kho, bhikkhu, anattato jānato passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.167:1.5: Rūpe anattato jānato passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.167:1.6: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ anattato jānato passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.167:1.7: Cakkhusamphassaṁ anattato jānato passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.167:1.8: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anattato jānato passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyati …pe…
root_text.sn35.167:1.9: jivhaṁ anattato jānato passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyati …pe…
root_text.sn35.167:1.10: manaṁ anattato jānato passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.167:1.14: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anattato jānato passato attānudiṭṭhi pahīyatī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.167:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn35.167:1.3: “Sir, how does one know and see so that theory of self is given up?”
translation_text.sn35.167:1.4: “Mendicant, knowing and seeing the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact as not-self, theory of self is given up. …
translation_text.sn35.167:1.14: And also knowing and seeing the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises conditioned by mind contact as not-self, theory of self is given up.”
